# About Worker's Compensation Liability Tracking: Canada

 You can track subcontractors that do not have worker's
 compensation clearance certificates so that you can identify those for whom you need to pay
 worker's compensation liabilities.
When you hire subcontractors without a valid
 worker's compensation clearance certificate, you are held responsible for their worker's
 compensation payments. You can track whether a subcontractor has a valid clearance
 certificate in the AP Vendors form, and run the AP Worker Comp Liabilities for
 Subcontractors report to help you identify the dollars paid to the subcontractor. You can
 then calculate and pay the worker's compensation liability.
To identify subcontractors as having valid clearance
 certificates, select the Valid Clearance Certificate checkbox in
 the AP Vendors form (Add'l Info tab). You can then enter the certificate number in the
 Clearance Cert
 # field and the certificate's effective date in the Effective Date
 field. Once you have selected the Valid Clearance Certificate checkbox,
 the subcontractor will not display on the AP Worker Comp Liabilities for Subcontractors
 report.
